pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take:
**I think it's astonishing how "The Noisemaker," who evidently picked his teams using a dartboard, finds himself at the top of the standings.** Look, I empathize with Devin—bless his heart—but when you draft Haiti in the fourth tier, what are you really hoping for? Spain's 0-0 draw against Cape Verde is another glaring example. I get why people say Rafe just got lucky; when you never even bother checking if your teams have qualified, and still lead the points, there's no other way to explain it. 

Now, let's ponder Gunner's "Bubba G" strategy of drafting both France and Senegal and placing them in the same group. It's not like they're in exceptional form, but I guess that's one way to ensure you won't lose points on match day. As for Zach, also known as "Mustard Boy," well, Austria facing off against Jordan feels a bit like watching two people throw wet noodles at a wall. My money's on Austria, but only because I've seen them play once and Jordan's just, you know, there. As confident as I sound—I mean, I have to be—I'm just not sure how "The Backpass Assassin" Gayden can turn things around with Jordan in his lineup. Good luck, folks, you're gonna need it.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's Perfect Storm: France Set to Dominate Senegal
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G competes against himself; who knew self-sabotage was a strategy?

pundit_takes.headline: Devin's Norway Set to Face Unfamiliar Foe in Iraq
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper confidently unaware if Iraq even plays soccer.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Austria and Gayden's Jordan Poised for Sleeper Matchup
pundit_takes.subtitle: Spectators expect riveting clash, which might actually just help with insomnia.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Leads Standings with Three Non-Qualifying Teams
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker strategy: ignorance clearly beats expertise in this World Cup.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe leads without qualifying any teams—chaos rookie is now chaos leader.
Rafe's Tier-1 duds didn’t stop him from laughing all the way to first.
Gayden owns Rafe in head-to-head, but can't ground this teenage dynamo in standings.
Sim couldn't predict a 15-year-old rookie dismantling seasoned veterans—keep your crystal ball, nerds.
